narration: Your car insurance company charges new customers less than you. The longer you stay, the more you overpay — it's called the loyalty penalty.
visual: Car SVG with two price tags: 'NEW CUSTOMER $1,200' and 'YOU $1,771', red arrow pointing at the higher price, title 'LOYALTY TAX' in red

narration: Studies show loyal auto insurance customers pay 19% more than brand-new ones. That's not a coincidence — that's policy. They count on you not noticing.
visual: Bar chart comparing NEW (short bar, green) vs YOU (taller bar, red) with '19% MORE' label and red upward arrow, person silhouette looking away

narration: Do the math. The average American pays $1,771 a year for car insurance. That 19% loyalty penalty costs you $847 every single year you don't act.
visual: Dollar counter animating to $847 labeled 'LOYALTY TAX / YEAR', calendar icon, decade total badge '$8,470' springing in below

narration: Why do they do it? Because most people never call. Every renewal you skip is another year of silent overpayment — and they're banking on your inertia.
visual: Phone SVG with speech bubble showing auto-renew notification, piggy bank with coins draining out, 'AUTO-RENEWED' badge in red

narration: Here's the fix — one fifteen-minute call. Tell them you got a competing quote, or just ask for their loyalty discount. Most people get $300 to $500 off immediately.
visual: Phone handset SVG with green speech bubble '$300–$500 OFF', checkmark, two competing quote papers side by side labeled YOUR INSURER vs COMPETITOR

narration: Over a decade that's $8,470 back in your pocket from one call you've been putting off. Follow for more money moves the industry hopes you never learn.
visual: Stack of coins growing to represent $8,470, person silhouette with arms raised, red 'FOLLOW FOR MORE' CTA badge with glow
